Title: Add New User
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend (${BACK_URL})
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security module
2. Click Add User
3. Enter user details (username, email, password)
4. Select account type
5. Click Save
Expected Result:
- User is created successfully
- User appears in user list
- Account type is set correctly

Title: Add User with Already Existing Login
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, at least one user exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security module
2. Click Add User
3. Enter username that already exists
4. Enter other required fields
5. Click Save
Expected Result:
- User is NOT created
- Error message displayed: 'Login already exists'
- User does not appear in list

Title: Edit User - Login
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test user exists (C172798)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open existing user
3. Change Login field
4. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Login is updated successfully
- User can login with new username

Title: Edit User - Email
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test user exists (C172798)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open existing user
3. Change Email field
4. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Email is updated successfully
- Email change reflected in user profile

Title: Edit User - StoreId
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test user exists (C172798)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open existing user
3. Change StoreId field
4. Click Save
Expected Result:
- StoreId is updated successfully
- User assigned to correct store

Title: Edit User - Account Type
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test user exists (C172798)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open existing user
3. Change Account Type (e.g., Manager to Administrator)
4. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Account type is updated successfully
- User permissions reflect new account type

Title: Search User in List View
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, multiple users exist including 'user_1'
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Enter search term 'user_1' in search field
3. Verify results
Expected Result:
- Only users matching 'user_1' are shown in list
- Search filters correctly

Title: Delete User
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test user 'user_1' exists (C172798)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Select user 'user_1'
3. Click Delete
4. Confirm deletion
Expected Result:
- User is deleted successfully
- User disappears from user list
- User cannot login

Title: Lock User Account
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test user 'user_1' exists (C172798)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open user 'user_1'
3. Click 'Lock Account' button
4. Save changes
5. Attempt to login as locked user at storefront
Expected Result:
- Account status changed to 'Locked'
- Login attempt displays: 'This account has been locked out, please try again later.'

Title: Unlock User Account
Preconditions: Locked user account exists (C179518)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Backend > Security
2. Find locked user from preconditions
3. Click 'Unlock account'
4. Save changes
5. Go to storefront and login as the user
Expected Result:
- Account status changed from 'Locked' to 'Unlocked'
- User can successfully login at storefront

Title: Add API Key
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, user account exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open user account
3. Navigate to API Keys widget
4. Click Add API Key
5. Enter key name
6. Click Generate
Expected Result:
- API key is generated successfully
- API key displayed with secret
- Key appears in API Keys list

Title: Reset API Key
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, API key 'apikey_2' exists (C184235)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open user with API key
3. Navigate to API Keys widget
4. Select 'apikey_2'
5. Click Reset
6. Confirm reset
Expected Result:
- API key 'apikey_2' is reset with new secret
- Old secret no longer valid
- New secret displayed

Title: Delete API Key
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, API key 'apikey_1' exists (C184235)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open user with API key
3. Navigate to API Keys widget
4. Select 'apikey_1'
5. Click Delete
6. Confirm deletion
Expected Result:
- API key 'apikey_1' is deleted
- Key no longer appears in list
- Key cannot be used for API calls

Title: API Key Active TRUE - Unlocked Account Returns Results
Preconditions: User account unlocked, API key exists with Active=TRUE (C172798, C184235)
Steps:
1. Open browser
2. Enter API endpoint URL with valid api_key parameter
3. Execute API request (e.g., /api/platform/security/users)
4. Observe response
Expected Result:
- JSON response is returned successfully
- Data is displayed in browser window
- No authentication errors

Title: API Key Active FALSE - Unlocked Account Returns No Results
Preconditions: User account unlocked, API key exists with Active=FALSE
Steps:
1. Set API key Active flag to FALSE in backend
2. Open browser
3. Enter API endpoint URL with api_key parameter
4. Execute API request
5. Observe response
Expected Result:
- JSON response is NOT returned
- Authentication error displayed
- Access denied

Title: API Key Active TRUE - Locked Account Returns No Results
Preconditions: User account locked (C179518), API key exists with Active=TRUE
Steps:
1. Verify account is locked in backend
2. Open browser
3. Enter API endpoint URL with api_key parameter
4. Execute API request
5. Observe response
Expected Result:
- JSON response is NOT returned
- Authentication error displayed
- Account locked message

Title: API Key Active FALSE - Locked Account Returns No Results
Preconditions: User account locked (C179518), API key exists with Active=FALSE
Steps:
1. Verify account is locked and API key inactive
2. Open browser
3. Enter API endpoint URL with api_key parameter
4. Execute API request
5. Observe response
Expected Result:
- JSON response is NOT returned
- Authentication error displayed
- Access denied

Title: Delete Account with API Key - Key Invalidated
Preconditions: User account exists with active API key (C172798, C184235)
Steps:
1. Note API key secret for later test
2. Navigate to Security > Users
3. Delete user account with API key
4. Confirm deletion
5. Attempt API request with previous api_key
Expected Result:
- User account deleted successfully
- API key no longer exists in system
- API request with previous key returns authentication error
- No results returned

Title: Add New Role
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Roles
2. Click Add Role
3. Enter role name (e.g., 'ContentEditor')
4. Enter description
5. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Role is added successfully
- Role appears in roles list
- Role available for assignment

Title: Edit Role
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test role exists (C172925)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Roles
2. Open existing role
3. Modify role name and/or description
4. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Role is updated successfully
- Changes reflected in roles list

Title: Delete Role
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, test role exists (C172925)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Roles
2. Select role to delete
3. Click Delete
4. Confirm deletion
Expected Result:
- Role is deleted successfully
- Role disappears from roles list
- Role no longer available for assignment

Title: Assign Permission to Role
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, role exists (C172925)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Roles
2. Open role
3. Navigate to Permissions widget
4. Select permission (e.g., 'catalog:read')
5. Click Assign
6. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Permission is assigned to role successfully
- Permission appears in role's permission list
- Users with this role inherit permission

Title: Remove Permission from Role
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, role with permissions exists (C172930)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Roles
2. Open role with assigned permissions
3. Navigate to Permissions widget
4. Select permission to remove
5. Click Remove
6. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Permission is removed from role successfully
- Permission no longer appears in role's permission list
- Users with this role no longer have permission

Title: Assign Role to User
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, user and role exist (C172798, C172925)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open user account
3. Navigate to Roles widget
4. Click Assign Role
5. Select role from dropdown
6. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Role is assigned to user successfully
- Roles widget shows updated count
- User inherits role permissions

Title: Edit Role Assignment for User
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, user with assigned roles exists (C184070)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open user account
3. Navigate to Roles widget
4. Change assigned roles (add/remove)
5. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Role assignment updated successfully
- Roles widget shows updated count
- User permissions updated accordingly

Title: Delete Roles from User
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, user with assigned roles exists (C184070)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Security > Users
2. Open user account
3. Navigate to Roles widget
4. Select role to remove
5. Click Remove
6. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Roles removed from user successfully
- Roles widget shows updated count (decreased)
- User no longer has removed role permissions

Title: Vendor Dynamic Property - Value Types Validation
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, Vendor exists
Steps:
1. Create Short Text property (Required): Verify required validation
2. Create Short Text property (Multivalue): Add multiple values
3. Create Short Text property (Multilingual): Add translations (EN, DE, FR)
4. Create Short Text property (Dictionary): Add dictionary values, select
5. Create Long Text property (Required + Multilingual): Test validation
6. Create Integer property (Required): Enter integer, test validation
7. Create Integer property (Multivalue): Add multiple integers
8. Test large integer value (boundary)
Expected Result:
- Short Text properties validate correctly (required, multivalue, multilingual, dictionary)
- Long Text properties support required + multilingual
- Integer properties validate type and boundaries
- Display order affects property order in UI
- Localization working for multilingual properties

Title: Healthcheck - No Problems (Modules)
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, all modules healthy
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Healthcheck (or /health endpoint)
2. Review overall system status
3. Review modules section
4. Verify all modules listed
5. Verify module health status
Expected Result:
- Status: Healthy
- All modules showing green/healthy status
- No errors or warnings displayed
- System operational

Title: Healthcheck - With Module Problems
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, at least one module with broken dependency installed
Steps:
1. Install module with broken dependency
2. Navigate to More > Healthcheck (or /health endpoint)
3. Review overall system status
4. Review modules section
5. Verify problematic module highlighted
Expected Result:
- Status: Degraded
- Problematic module(s) highlighted with errors
- Error details displayed
- Other healthy modules still shown as healthy

Title: Healthcheck - No Problems (Cache)
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, Redis cache operational
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Healthcheck (or /health endpoint)
2. Review overall system status
3. Review cache section
4. Verify cache status (Redis)
5. Verify cache connectivity
Expected Result:
- Status: Healthy
- Cache showing green/healthy status
- Redis connection successful
- No cache errors

Title: Healthcheck - With Cache Problems
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend, cache unavailable or misconfigured
Steps:
1. Stop Redis service or break cache connection
2. Navigate to More > Healthcheck (or /health endpoint)
3. Review overall system status
4. Review cache section
5. Verify cache error displayed
Expected Result:
- Status: Degraded or Unhealthy
- Cache showing red/error status
- Error description displayed (e.g., 'Redis connection failed')
- System still partially operational
